{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents mention that Glanzmann's thrombasthenia is a congenital bleeding disorder caused by a deficiency of the platelet integrin alpha IIb beta3. This integrin is the platelet fibrinogen receptor and is essential to platelet aggregation and hemostasis. Therefore, Glanzmann disease is a congenital defect of platelets.", "answer_choice": "A"}
